Excessively Rare Broad 2.5 Taler in Marvelous Condition
Unlisted in Many Reference Works
    
14217
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. 2.5 Taler, ND. Friedrich III. NGC AU-55. Dav-cf. LS445/446; KM-cf. 
88/89; Sieg-13; Lange cf. 319; Bruun-14339. Weight: 73.21 gms. KM lists a 1.5 and a 3.5 Taler, both as rare, but the pres-
ent 2.5 Taler is unlisted in both KM, Lange and Davenport. A touch of doubling on both sides as so often on these large 
silver issues does nothing to distract from the amazing condition of this exceptionally rare Broad 2.5 Taler. The detailing 
on the Duke’s armor is exquisite. A once in a lifetime (or, indeed, several lifetimes) opportunity.
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: C. J. Thomsen Collection (Copenhagen - 1867-1876), Lot # 2546.

Charming Portrait and Wonderful Details to Reverse

GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Ducat, 1664. Christian Albrecht. NGC Unc Details—Bent. Fr-3080; KM-
109; Lange-371C; Sieg-10.1; Bruun-14361. Weight: 3.48 gms. Nicely detailed with bright luster. Extremely rare - Lange 
only had a galvanic copy.
€5,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Frederik Chr. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906/1907) Lot # 1629.
CHRISTIAN JURGENSEN THOMSEN (1788-1865)  
Christian Jurgensen Thomsen (1788-1865) was an interna -
tionally famous antiquarian, numismatist and director of the 
National Museum of Denmark. He is considered the origina-
tor of the three-age system of European antiquity, dividing 
pre-history into the Stone, Bronze and Iron Ages. Thomsen 
was also a prominent numismatist, and in many ways his col-
lection of European medieval coins has never been surpassed.


========== PAGE 298 ==========

Page 272 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
Very Rarely Seen in Such Pleasing Condition
14219
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Taler, 1673-MF. Hamburg Mint. Christian Albrecht. NGC AU-55. Dav-
3703; KM-118; Lange-374; Sieg-7.1; Bruun-14417. Weight: 29.07 gms. Mintmaster: Matthias Freude the Y ounger. An 
extremely rare and absolutely stunning coin with rich golden luster.
Matthias Freude was born in Rostock in 1635 and became mintmaster in Lübeck 1662. In 1668 he moved to Hamburg and 
in June 1673 he left his position. We must therefore assume this Taler was minted in the first half of 1673.
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: A. C. Mohr Collection (Copenhagen - 1847) Lot # 3711.

========== PAGE 304 ==========

Page 278 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
A Most Magnificent Ducat of the Utmost Rarity and Importance
14227
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Ducat, 1698. Tönning Mint. Friedrich IV . NGC AU-58. Fr-3083; KM-73; 
Lange-unlisted; Sieg-12.7; Bruun-14490. Weight: 3.38 gms. Mintmaster: Hans Heinrich Lüders. Alluring coin with rich 
luster. Extremely rare.
Interesting type with depiction of Stabelholm Fortress on the reverse. The fort was placed between the rivers Ejderen and 
Trene close to the current city of Friederichstadt. Friederichstadt was founded by Duke Friederich III in 1621 where the two 
rivers meet.
The restrike ordered at The Royal Mint by J. Wilcke in 1941 and sold at his auction in 1946 (Part IV No 191) was struck from 
the dies for the 4 and 8 Schillings and not from the Ducat dies. This Ducat is from the original dies.
€8,000-€10,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
14228
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. 8 Schilling, 1698. Tönning Mint. Friedrich IV . NGC MS-64. KM-164; 
Lange-430B; Sieg-5; Bruun-14491. Weight: 5.72 gms. Lovely coin with attractive toning and crisp details.
The 4 and 8 Schillings with the image of Stapelholm Fortress was minted with different dies than the Ducat from the same 
year. 
€1,000-€1,500
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: F. C. Bech Collection (Copenhagen - 1906/1907) Lot # 1671.

14240
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Ducat, 1712-BH. Tönning Mint. Karl Friedrich. NGC AU-58. Fr-unlist-

14241
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp. Taler, 1753-S/P . Mannheim Mint. Karl Peter Ulrich (T sar Peter III). NGC

Ploss. Engraver: Wigand Schäffer. A superb Mint State example of this highly coveted Taler from the Duke who later 
became Tsar of Russia.
Karl Peter Ulrich of Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp inherited the Russian Throne in 1762 but was overthrown and murdered 
by his wife, Catherine (the Great) the same year. Catherine ruled Russia until her death in 1796.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 312 ==========

Page 286 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
    
14242

inhardt. Struck in the year of his death, this Taler is exceptionally well-preserved for type and would be the highlight of 
any collection - and the envy of many.

Excessively Rare Half Death Taler
Only a Handful in Private Ownership
14246
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg. 1/2 Taler, 1622. Reinfeld Mint. Johann the Y ounger. NGC EF-45. 
KM-17; Sieg-20; JSJ-24; Lange-545; Bruun-14666. Weight: 14.19 gms. Mintmaster: Tobias Reinhardt (orb). Incredibly 
rare Half Death Taler. Much rarer than both the whole and double Death Talers with very few examples known- possibly 
only three in private collections.
The ink number written on the obverse refers to Madai’s Thaler-Cabinett from 1765, a book which was for a long time 
used as a reference.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 316 ==========

Page 290 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
    
14247
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Sonderburg. Taler, 1626. 
Reinfeldt or Sonderburg Mint. Alexander. NGC VF-30. Dav-
3716; KM-26; Sieg-2; JSJ-30; Lange-558; Bruun-14677. Weight: 
28.52 gms. Mintmaster: Johann Lilienthal. Extremely rare por -
trait Taler of Alexander, son of Johann (Hans) the Y ounger. He 
reigned for only 5 years (1622-1627). Excellent toning over sur-
faces that are surely much better than the numeric grade; the 
coin does have some weakness in its central areas which may 
explain the somewhat harsh grading. This is the only large por-
trait coin of Alexander. 
€4,000-€5,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: H. M. Mayntzhusen Collection (Bought en bloc by the National 
Museum in Copenhagen who sold off duplicates in public auction in 
1883).
    
14248
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Glücksburg. Taler, 1632. 
Philip. NGC EF Details—Cleaned. Dav-3718; KM-5; Sieg-1; 
Lange-736a; Bruun-14680. Weight: 28.85 gms. Mintmaster: To-
bias Reinhardt(?). Very rare one-year type with an eye-appeal 
which far surpasses the grade on the holder. 
€4,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
14249
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Glücksburg. Taler, 1672. 
Glücksburg Mint. Christian. NGC AU-55. Dav-unlisted; KM-
unlisted; Sieg-1; Lange 737; Bruun-14681. Weight: 28.77 gms. 
Dusty yet colorful, original toning covers the surfaces on this 
impressible Broad Taler for a most enchanting look.
Struck on the death of Sibylla Ursula, Wife of Duke Christian of 
Schleswig-Holstein-Glücksburg and daughter of Duke August of 
Braunschweig-Wolfenbüttel. The latter is often given as the issuer, 
but opinions seem to be divided. We have chosen to remain faithful 
to Bruun and Lange. However, in one instance we must disagree 
with them both: They write (and all others do, too) the date as 1671 
when the correct date must be 1672 since the burial date is given 
as February 6, 1672. The last ‘I’ in the Latin date MDCLXXII is al-
most missing in the edge, hence presumably the misinterpretation. 
€1,800-€2,200
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: August Chr. Mohr Collection (Copenhagen - 1847), Lot # 3848.

Lustrous Ducat
Possibly Only Four Known
14250
GERMANY . Schleswig-Holstein-Glücksburg. Ducat, 1716. Philip Ernst. NGC AU-58. Fr-3074; KM-50; Lange-747; 
Bruun-14682. Weight: 3.47 gms. Excessively rare with possibly only four known. Very lightly bent, lustrous and well-
struck. 
While the mint is unknown, the occasion for the minting is not: The reverse shield is surrounded by the Danish Order of the 
Elephant, which was bestowed upon Philipp Ernst in Copenhagen on November 16, 1716.

From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 319 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 293
Session II • Saturday , March 15, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Extremely Rare Ducal Emission
14255
DENMARK. Schleswig-Holstein. Mark, 1514. Husum Mint. Frederik I. NGC EF Details—Cleaned. Galster-115; 
Sieg-46; Schou-2; Lange-11; Bruun-14708. Weight: 18.99 gms. Mintmaster: Jørgen Drewes. This iconic Mark with Saint 
Andreas and the legend “ Delixit Dominus Andream (The Lord loved Andreas)” is an extremely rare and sought after 
issue no matter the grade.
Frederik I was born 7th of October 1471 as the younger son of Christian I and Dorothea of Brandenburg. After the death of 
his father King Christian I, he was elected Duke of Schleswig-Holstein in 1482 in Kiel. His older brother and co-Duke Hans 
(John) was elected King of Denmark in 1483. Frederik married Anna of Brandenburg in 1502 and fathered two children, 
Christian 1503 (later Christian III) and Dorothea of Denmark. Anna died in 1514, 26 years old. In 1518 he married Sophie 
of Pomerania and with her fathered six children.
Frederik I was proclaimed King of Denmark in Viborg 1523 and crowned in Copenhagen 1524 and he was the last Catholic 
King of Denmark. He died in Gottorp Castle 10 of April 1533 and was interred in Schleswig Cathedral.
€30,000-€40,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

========== PAGE 320 ==========

Page 294 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
Ducal Emission of the Greatest Rarity and Most Pleasing Condition
14256

Sieg-45; Schou-3; not in Lange; Bruun-14709. Weight: 9.58 gms. Mintmaster: Jørgen Drewes. Excessively rare 1/2 Mark 
with the depiction of Saint Andreas. A lovely coin with alluring golden toning.

========== PAGE 321 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 295
Session II • Saturday , March 15, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Superbly Well-Preserved and Extremely Rare Ducal Emission 
Struck at Gottorp Mint
14257
DENMARK. Goldgulden (Rhinsk Gulden), ND (1523-1533). Gottorp Mint. Frederik I. NGC AU-58. Fr-14; Gal-
ster-122; Sieg-49; Lange-9a; Schou-1; Bruun-14741. Weight: 3.24 gms. The obverse shows Saint Andreas (Andrew) with 
the shield of Holstein beneath. Saint Andrew became a martyr after he was crucified in 60 AD in the Greek city of Patras 
located in the northern part of Peloponnese. Reverse with the shields of Holstein, Schleswig, Norway and Stormarn with 
center shield of Oldenburg. Extremely rare and intriguing coin with old gold toning, minted after the Rhenish standard.
Gold Guldens were minted by the cities of Cologne, Mainz, Trier from ca. 1354 onwards.
In St. Peter’s Basilica in Rome there are four shrines surrounding the high altar. The shrines contain the most holy relics of 
Christendom. Namely, the largest fragment of the holy cross, the miraculous veil of St. Veronica, the lance that pierced Jesus 
and the skull of Saint Andrew. The last shrine is however empty as the skull of Saint Andrew was returned to Patras in 1964 
by Pope Paul VI as a gesture to the Greek Orthodox Church.
€22,000-€26,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Countship of Brahesminde Collection (Private Sale - 1922).

========== PAGE 322 ==========

Page 296 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
Extremely Rare Ducal Emission of the  
Greatest Historical Importance
14258
DENMARK. Schleswig-Holstein. Goldgulden (Rhinsk Gulden), 1536. Roskilde Mint. Christian III. NGC AU De-
tails—Edge Filing. Fr-18; Galster-131; Sieg-23; Schou-4; Lange-18; Bruun-14770. Weight: 3.37 gms. Extremely rare, 
and a piece that will catch much attention, even with the edge filing.
This historical interesting Goldgulden was minted during the civil war called Grevens Fejde (Count’s Feud).
Although the legend on the reverse states “MON NOVA A VREIA SLESVICENSIS” this coin was probably minted in 
Roskilde. A payment of around 350 ounces of gold was received at Copenhagen Castle on November 30, 1536 and trans-
ported to Roskilde to be minted into Goldgulden as payment for the troops.
€10,000-€15,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

14259
DENMARK. Schleswig-Holstein. Ducat, ND (1534). Gottorp Mint. Christian III. NGC AU Details—Obverse Dam-

verse with the shields of Holstein, Schleswig, Stormarn and Norway, center shield of Oldenburg. Reverse with Orb. 
Extremely rare, and a desirable type, even with the damage.
This Goldgulden was the last issue minted by Reynold Junge in 1534 in his last year as mintmaster in Gottorp.
€18,000-€22,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

The Younger brother of Friedrich IV of Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp, Christian Albert was given the small duchy of Eutin 
and was elected Bishop of Lübeck in 1706. He served as co-ruler in Schleswig-Holstein-Gottorp from the death of his brother 
until the coming-of-age of his nephew, Karl Friedrich, in 1717.

The Very First Taler from Oldenburg
Extremely Rare and in Excellent Condition
14278
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, ND (1538). Anton. NGC EF-40. Dav-9606; Trippler/Kalvelage-119; Bruun-15088. 
Weight: 28.41 gms. Extremely rare and the first Taler from Oldenburg. Trippler/Kalvelage reports 9 (not including this). 
Immensely pleasing with an antique feel from the deep toning that melds perfectly with the consistent wear.
€15,000-€18,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
14279
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Ducat, 1664. Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC AU-55. Fr-1930; KM-63; Trippler/
Kalvelage-143; Bruun-15097. Weight: 3.46 gms. Mintmaster: Georg David Ziegenhorn. The excellent surfaces, lustrous 
and inviting as they are, truly makes this coin stand out. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 338 ==========

Page 312 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
    
14280
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, 1665-IGP/IGP . Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC AU-53. Dav-7115; KM-84; Trip-
pler/Kalvelage-145; Bruun-15099. Weight: 28.88 gms. Mintmaster: Georg David Ziegenhorn. Engraver: Unknown. 
Struck commemorating Count Anton Günther’s 82nd birthday, this handsome Taler shows the count as aged but still 
imposing; excellent detailing in especially the hair and the delicately toned legend. 
€2,500-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Aug. Chr. Mohr Collection (Copenhagen - 1847), Lot # 3910.
    
14281
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, 1666. Anton Günther. NGC AU-55. Dav-7116; KM-86; Trippler/Kalvelage-149; Bru-
un-15100. Weight: 28.89 gms. Mintmaster: Georg David Ziegenhorn. Struck commemorating Count Anton Günter’s 
83rd (and last) birthday. Excellent toning and hints of luster on this high graded, imposing Taler. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Aug. Chr. Mohr Collection (Copenhagen - 1847), Lot # 3911.
    
14282
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, 1667. Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC EF-45. Dav-7118; KM-93; Trippler/
Kalvelage-306; Bruun-15101. Weight: 27,52 gms. Mintmaster: Georg David Ziegenhorn. Struck on the death of the 
count. Pleasing, old toning on this extremely rare Death Taler. 
€5,000-€6,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 339 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 313
Session II • Saturday , March 15, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
Hefty and Thoroughly Impressive Double Taler Klippe
Unbelievably Beautiful and Excessively Rare
     
   
14283
GERMANY . Oldenburg. 2 Taler Klippe, ND (1614-1619). Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC MS-64. Dav-7101; KM-
14; Kalvelage/Trippler-130; Bruun-15106. Weight: 59.05 gms. Mintmaster: Nikolaus Wintgens. Excessively rare Double 
Klippe Taler in the most amazing condition. With only a handful known, this Mint State-64 Klippe is among the most 
magnificent in this auction - which is quite a feat considering the competition.
€20,000-€25,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Mikocki Collection (Vienna - 1850), Lot # 3427.


========== PAGE 340 ==========

Page 314 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II
Sensationally Preserved Taler Klippe
14284
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler Klippe, ND (1614-1619). Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC MS-63. Dav-7104; KM-7; 
Trippler/Kalvelage-150; Bruun-15107. Weight: 29.15 gms. Mintmaster: Nikolaus Wintgen. Very rare Klippe struck with 
Half Taler dies. As with so many coins from the Bruun Collection this is in the most astounding condition.
Davenport number listed on NGC holder is incorrect.
€6,000-€8,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
Ex: Aug. Chr. Mohr Collection (Copenhagen - 1847), Lot # 3913.

========== PAGE 341 ==========

 Bid Online at www.StacksBowers.com P age 315
Session II • Saturday , March 15, 2025 • 2:00  PM CET / 6:00  AM PT
    
14285
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, ND (1614-1622). Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC AU-55. Dav-7107; KM-9; Trip-
pler/Kalvelage-137a; Bruun-15108. Weight: 29.06 gms. Mintmaster: Nicolaus Wintgens. Excellent condition for this rare 
Taler, struck in Jever, the administrative city of present day Lower Saxony (Niedersachsen). 
€2,500-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
    
14286
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Taler, ND (1619-1622). Jever Mint. Anton Günther. NGC EF-45. Dav-7109; KM-12; Trip-
pler/Kalvelage-unlisted (140D for obverse); Bruun-15109. Weight: 28.78 gms. Mintmaster: Nicolaus Wintgens. This 
reverse legend is not one of the five variations listed in Trippler/Kalvelage. Excellent, toned Taler with the crest of the 
Holy Roman Emperor on the reverse. 
€2,000-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.

========== PAGE 342 ==========

Page 316 Stack’s Bowers Galleries
Superb and Rare Mint State Ducat
Only 580 Struck
14287
GERMANY . Oldenburg. Lübeck (Bishopric). 5 Taler (Pistole - Friederich Augustd’ or), 1776-B. Altona Mint. Fried-
rich August. NGC MS-61. Fr-1509 and 1931; KM-87 (Lübeck); Trippler/Kalvelage-369; Lange-522; Bruun-15142. 
Weight: 6.66 gms. Mintmaster: Casper Heinrich Lyng. Engraver: Johann Ephraim Bauert. Very rare, only 580 struck; 
200 in 1776 and a further 380 in 1781 using the same dies, making any distinction impossible. Superb condition, the 
second highest ranking on the NGC census.
Struck by Friedrich August as both Duke of Oldenburg and Bishop of Lübeck. His coinage can therefore be found listed 
under either. Friedberg has chosen to list this coin under both, hence the two reference numbers.
€2,500-€3,000
From the L. E. Bruun Collection.
End of Session 2
The L.  E. Bruun Co llection • A Corpus of Scandinavian Monetary History Part II

TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF SALE
1. Auction Basics
This is a public auction sale (“Auction Sale”) conducted by 
auctioneer, Stack’s Bowers Galleries (hereinafter referred 
to as “Auctioneer” and at times as “Stack’s Bowers”). Bid-
ding in this Auction Sale constitutes acceptance by you 
(“Bidder”) of all the Terms and Conditions of Sale stated 
herein (the “Terms of Sale”). These Terms of Sale are also 
available on the website https://stacksbowers.com.
Lots are auctioned on behalf and for the account of the 
consignors who have consigned lots with Stack’s Bowers 
pursuant to a consignment agreement (the “ Consignor” 
or “Consignors”). Bidders acknowledge that Stack’s Bow-
ers might receive a commission from the Consignor(s) for 
the sale. 
Bidders may include Consignors who may bid and pur -
chase lots in the Auction Sale consigned by the Consignor 
pursuant to their consignment agreement with Stack’s 
Bowers. A Consignor that bids on their own lots in the 
Auction Sale may pay a different fee than the Buyer’s Pre-
mium charged to all other Buyers. 
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to include in any auc -
tion sale its own material as well as material from affili -
ated or related companies, principals, officers or employ-
ees. Stack’s Bowers may have direct or indirect interests 
in any of the lots in the auction and may collect commis-
sions. THE TWO PRECEDING SENTENCES SHALL 
BE DEEMED A PART OF THE DESCRIPTION OF ALL 
LOTS CONTAINED IN THE CATALOG. 
Where the Consignor has repurchased a lot and the lot is 
either returned to the Consignor or otherwise dealt with 
or disposed of in accordance with the Consignor’s direc-
tion, or pursuant to contractual agreement, Stack’s Bow-
ers reserves the right to so note in the prices realized or to 
omit a price from the prices realized. 
Lots may carry a reserve (“Reserve”). A Reserve is a price or 
bid below which the Auctioneer will not sell an item or will 
repurchase an item on behalf of the Consignor or for Stack’s 
Bowers. Reserves may be confidential and not disclosed. 
The Auctioneer has discretion to withdraw any unsold lots 
at any time during the Auction Sale without third parties 
being aware. Once the Auction Sale is closed, enquiries 
can be submitted to the Auctioneer concerning unsold 
lots. No participant in the Auction Sale shall have a right 
to claim any damages, including consequential damages 
if a lot is withdrawn, even if the withdrawal occurs after 
the Auction Sale.
The Buyer is the Bidder who makes the highest bid ac -
cepted by the Auctioneer, and includes the principal of 
any Bidder acting as an agent. Auctioneer reserves the 
right to cancel or postpone the Auction Sale or any ses-
sion thereof for any reason whatsoever, and no Bidder 
shall have any claim as a result of any such cancellation 
or postponement.
2. Descriptions and Grading
Bidder acknowledges that grading of most coins in this 
Auction Sale have been determined by independent grad-
ing services, and those that are not graded by an inde -
pendent grading service may be graded by Stack’s Bow -
ers. Grading of rare coins is subjective and, even though 
grading has a material effect on the value of the coins, 
grading may differ among independent grading services 
and among numismatists. Stack’s Bowers is not respon -
sible for the grades assigned by independent grading ser-
vices, and makes no warranty or representation regarding 
such grades. Bidder further acknowledges and agrees that 
grades assigned by Stack’s Bowers and lot descriptions are 
based solely upon an examination of the coins and are 
intended solely to identify coins and note any perceived 
characteristics. However, coin grading and descriptions 
by Stack’s Bowers are subjective. Stack’s Bowers does not 
warrant the accuracy of such grading or descriptions, nor 
do they in any way form the basis for any bid. Unless not-
ed, all photographs in this catalog are of the actual items 
being sold but may not be the actual size or scale.
3. The Bidding Process
The Auctioneer shall have the right to open or accept bid-
ding on any lot by placing a bid on behalf of: the Consign-
or or his or her agent; a mail, telephone, Internet or telefax 
Bidder; or any other participant in the Auction Sale. 
Bids must be for an entire lot and each lot constitutes a 
separate sale. All bids must be on an increment or half 
increment (a cut bid) as established by the Auctioneer. 
Non-conforming bids will be rounded down to the near-
est half or full increment and this rounded bid will be the 
Bidder’s high bid. 
No lot will be broken up unless otherwise permitted by the 
Auctioneer. Lots will be sold in their numbered sequence 
unless Auctioneer directs otherwise. All material shall be 
sold in separate lots to the highest Bidder as determined 
by the Auctioneer. Auctioneer shall have the right in its 
sole and absolute discretion to accept or decline any bid, 
establish bid increments, challenge any bid or bidding in-
crement, to reduce any mail bid received, adjudicate all 
bidding disputes, to exclude any Bidder and to determine 
the prevailing bid. The Auctioneer shall have the right, but 
not the obligation, to rescind the acceptance of any bid 
and place the lot(s) for Auction Sale again. 
For Bidder’s protection, no “unlimited” or “buy” bids will 
be accepted. When identical bids are received for a lot, 
preference is given to the first bid received as determined 
by the Auctioneer. For example, a mail bid will take prece-
dence over an identical floor bid or live internet bid. 
Cut bids are only accepted on bids greater than €500 and 
each Bidder may only execute one cut bid per lot. 
All bids are final and cannot be cancelled or withdrawn 
without the express consent of the Auctioneer, in its sole 
discretion. 
Bids will not be accepted from persons who are obviously 
not capable of judgement or from persons under eighteen 
(18) years of age without a parent’s written consent which 
acknowledges the Terms of Sale herein and agrees to be 
bound thereby on behalf of the underage Bidder. 
The Auction Sale is complete when the Auctioneer so an-
nounces by the fall of the hammer or in any other custom-
ary manner. The Auctioneer is responsible for keeping re-
cords of the Auction Sale. 
Auctioneer’s decision on all bidding disputes shall be 
binding and final. Article 13 of the Verordnung des Ober-

gerichts über das Verfahren bei freiwilligen öffentlichen 
Versteigerungen dated 19 December 1979 and article 230 
§1 of the Swiss Code of Obligations are reserved.
THIS IS NOT AN APPROV AL SALE. Except as other -
wise expressly provided in these Terms of Sale, NO PUR -
CHASED ITEMS MAY BE RETURNED FOR ANY REASON. 
All prospective Bidders who examine the lot(s) prior to 
the Auction Sale personally assume all responsibility for 
any damage that Bidder causes to the lot(s). Stack’s Bow-
ers shall have sole reasonable discretion in determining 
the value of the damage caused by a Bidder, which shall 
be promptly paid by such Bidder.
STACK’S BOWERS IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY 
ERRORS IN BIDDING. All Bidders should make certain 
to bid on the correct lot and that the bid is the bid intend-
ed. Once the hammer has fallen and the Auctioneer has 
announced the Buyer, the Buyer is unconditionally bound 
to pay for the lot, even if the Buyer made a mistake. 
4. Bidder Registration Required
ll persons seeking to bid must complete and sign a regis -
tration card either at the auction or online, or otherwise 
qualify to bid, as determined in the sole discretion of the 
Auctioneer. By submitting a bid, the Bidder acknowledges 
that Bidder has read the Terms of Sale, the descriptions 
for the lot(s) on which they have bid, and that they agree 
to be bound by these Terms of Sale. The Bidder acknowl-
edges that the invoice describing a lot by number incor -
porates the catalog and Terms of Sale. Person appearing 
on the U.S.A. ’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (“OFAC”) 
sanction list, or any comparable list issued by the Europe-
an Union, the United Nations, Switzerland or the country 
hosting the auction are not eligible to bid.
5. Buyer’s Premiums and Value Added Tax (V AT)
Lots will be temporarily admitted in Switzerland for the 
Auction Sale and shipped back at Stack’s Bowers risks to 
either Denmark or the U.S.A. immediately after the Auc-
tion Sale. Lots will then be delivered and shipped to Buy-
ers from Denmark or the U.S.A. 
A premium between twenty and twenty five percent (20-
25%) of the total amount of the hammer price (minimum 
of €25), will be added to all purchases of individual lots 
(the “Buyer’s Premium”).
Relevant clauses of the Danish and U.S.A. Terms of Sale 
of Stack’s Bowers shall apply regarding the amount of the 
Buyer’s Premium and the potential application of local 
V AT depending on where the lots will ultimately be ex -
ported. 
Swiss V AT will not apply and will not be invoiced by 
Stack’s Bowers to the Buyers as the lots will be shipped 
outside of Switzerland immediately after the Auction Sale. 
Buyer’s Premiums as described above will be applied re -
gardless of affiliation with any group or organization. A 
reacquisition charge may apply to Consignors pursuant to 
a separate agreement, which may be higher or lower than 
the Buyer’s Premium.
Any import (and/or turnover) tax and duty and any other 
taxes, fees and charges payable abroad is on Buyer’s ac-
count in every case. Buyer is responsible to obtain the 
necessary information and comply with any applicable 
customs regulations, foreign exchange regulations and 
any other regulations abroad. The Auctioneer shall not be 
liable for any contraventions of such regulations. 
Buyer’s attention is drawn to the fact that import to, and 
export from States that are parties to the Convention of 14 
November 1970 on the Means of Prohibiting and Prevent-
ing the Illicit Import, Export and Transfer of Ownership 
of Cultural Property (1970 UNESCO Convention) might 
be subject to specific regulations.
Buyer undertakes not to export, transfer or make avail -
able the purchased lots to countries and/or to individuals 
or entities that are subject to economic sanctions, embar-
goes or other trade restrictions imposed by the United 
States, the United Nations, the European Union, and the 
Swiss Confederation and to comply with all applicable 
regulations regarding export and sanctions. 
6. Payment
Payment is due immediately upon the fall of the Auction-
eer’s hammer. Payment is delinquent and in default if not 
received in full, in good funds, within fourteen (14) calen-
dar days of the Auction Sale (the “Default Date”), without 
exception, time being of the essence.
All invoices will be made in Euros. Unless otherwise 
agreed in writing prior to the Auction Sale, all auction 
sales are payable strictly in U.S. Dollars or Euros. If pay -
ment is made in U.S Dollars, Buyer’s invoices will be cred-
ited with the amount of Euros at the currency exchange 
rate established by the Auctioneer on the date of the 
Auction Sale. Provided, this exchange rate is only valid 
for payments made on the date of the Auction Sale and 
will thereafter fluctuate. Payments made in U.S. Dollars 
after the date of the Auction Sale will be credited with the 
lower of the currency exchange rate on the payment date 
or the date of the Auction Sale. Buyer will be responsible 
for payment of any shortfall upon receipt of notice from 
the Auctioneer of such shortfall amount. Payments may 
be made by U.S. Dollars or Euros, checks from U.S. banks, 
wire transfer or cashier’s check. Cash transactions will not 
be accepted. Contact the Auctioneer for wiring instruc-
tions before sending a wire. Wire transfers from banks lo-
cated outside of the United States are subject to an inter -
national bank wire fee of €35. Payment by ACH/eCheck 
will be accepted upon prior approval by Auctioneer. Pay-
ment by credit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express 
and Discover) or Paypal will be accepted upon prior ap -
proval by Auctioneer. All payments by credit card or Pay-
pal will incur a surcharge of 2.5%. This fee only applies to 
credit card or Paypal transactions, and does not exceed 
Auctioneer’s cost of processing these payments. Payment 
by check, ACH/eCheck, wire transfer or cashier’s check 
will not incur this surcharge. All payments are subject to a 
clearing period determined by the Auctioneer. 
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right not to release lots for 
which good funds have not yet been received in full. On 
any past due accounts, Stack’s Bowers reserves the right, 
without notice, to extend credit and impose carrying 
charges (as described below). Buyers agree to pay reason-
able attorney’s fees and costs incurred to collect past due 
accounts as permitted under applicable law. Any invoice

Buyers personally and unconditionally guarantee pay -

person by submitting bids on behalf of a corporation or 
other entity, by making such bid, agrees to be personally 
jointly and severally liable for the payment of the pur -

Buyer obligations under these Terms of Sale and Stack’s 
Bowers reserves the right to require a written guarantee 
of such payments and obligations. 
Bidders who have not established credit with Stack’s Bow-

other amount as Stack’s Bowers may, in its sole and ab -

to purchases. Any remaining deposits will be promptly 
refunded, upon clearance of funds.

make loans or advances to Consignors and/or Bidders.
7.  Sales Tax
United States Buyers will be charged all applicable sales 
tax unless a valid Resale Certificate has been provided to 
the Auctioneer prior to the auction. Should state sales tax 
become applicable in the delivery state prior to delivery 
of the property on the invoice, the United States Buyers 
agree to pay all applicable state sales tax as required by 
the delivery state as of the shipping date. In the event any 
applicable sales tax is not paid by a United States Buyer 
that should have been paid, even if not such tax was not 
charged or collected by Stack’s Bowers by mistake, error, 
negligence or gross negligence, a United States Buyer 
nonetheless acknowledges responsibility to pay such sales 
tax and remains fully liable for and agrees to promptly 
pay such taxes on demand, together with any interest or 
penalty that may be assessed by the taxing authority and 
agrees to indemnify and hold Auctioneer harmless from 
any applicable sales tax, interest or penalties due. Lots 
from different Auctions may not be aggregated for sales 
tax purposes.
8.  Financial Responsibility
In the event any applicable conditions of these Terms of 
Sale herein are not complied with by a Buyer or if the 
Buyer fails to make payment in full by the Default Date, 
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right, in its sole discretion, in 
addition to all other remedies which it may have at law or 
in equity to rescind the sale of that lot or any other lot or 
lots sold to the defaulting Buyer, retaining all payments 
made by Buyer as liquidated damages, it being recognized 
that actual damages may be speculative or difficult to 
compute, and upon prior notice to Buyer resell a portion 
or all of the lots held by Stack’s Bowers, in a commercially 
reasonable manner, which may include a public or private 
sale, in a quantity sufficient in the sole opinion of Stack’s 
Bowers to satisfy the indebtedness, plus all accrued charg-
es, and Stack’s Bowers may charge a seller’s commission 
that is commercially reasonable. More than one such sale 
may take place at the option of Stack’s Bowers. If Stack’s 
Bowers resells the lots, Buyer agrees to pay for the reason-
able cost of such sale. Notice of the sale shall be by inter -
nationally recognized overnight courier service to the ad-
dress utilized on the Bid Sheet, Auction Consignment and 
Security Agreement or other last known address by Stack’s 
Bowers. The proceeds shall be applied first to the satisfac-
tion of any damages occasioned by Buyer’s breach, then 
to any other indebtedness owed to Stack’s Bowers, includ-
ing without limitation, commissions, handling charges, 
carrying charges, the expenses of both sales, seller’s fees, 
reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs, collection agency fees 
and costs and any other costs or expenses incurred. Buyer 
may also be liable to Stack’s Bowers for any deficiency if 
the proceeds of such sale or sales are insufficient to cover 
such amounts.
Buyer grants to Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and assignees, 
the right to offset any sums due, or found to be due to 
Stack’s Bowers, and to make such offset from any sums 
due to Buyer by Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and assignees.
In addition, defaulting Buyers will be deemed to have 
granted to Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates and assignees, a 
security interest in: (x) the purchased lots and their pro -
ceeds, and (y) such sums or other items and their pro -
ceeds, in the possession of Stack’s Bowers, its affiliates or 
assignees, to secure all indebtedness due to Stack’s Bow -
ers and its affiliated companies, plus all accrued expenses, 
carrying charges, seller’s fees, attorney fees, and costs, un-
til the indebtedness is paid in full. A United States Buyer 
grants Stack’s Bowers the right to file a UCC-1 financing 
statement for such items. Non-United States Buyers grant 
to Stack’s Bowers such other form of and any necessary 
power of attorney and undertake to provide Stack’s Bow-
ers with any other information and/or documents as may 
be required to perfect or otherwise confirm a security in-
terest therein. Stack’s Bowers may assign any such inter-
est to any affiliated or related company or any third party 
deemed appropriate by Stack’s Bowers without notice or 
consent of the Buyer. 
If the auction invoice is not paid for in full by the De -
fault Date, a carrying charge of one-and-one-half per -
cent (1-1/2%) per month may be imposed on the unpaid 
amount until it is paid in full. In the event this interest 
rate exceeds the interest permitted by law, the same shall 
be adjusted to the maximum rate permitted by law, and 
any amount paid in excess thereof shall be allocated to 
principal. Where permitted under applicable law, Buyer 
agrees to pay all reasonable attorney’s fees, court costs and 
other collection costs incurred by Stack’s Bowers or any 
affiliated or related company to collect past due invoices 
or to interpret or enforce the terms hereof or in any ac -
tion or proceeding arising out of or related to the Auction 
Sale. Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to assign its interest 
to any third party. To the extent that the Buyer for any 
lot consists of more than one person or entity, each such 
person or entity is jointly and severally liable for all obli -
gations of the Buyer, regardless of the title or capacity of 
such person or entity. With respect to United States Buy-

ers, Stack’s Bowers shall have all the rights of a secured 
creditor under Article 9 of the California Commercial 
Code and with respect to non-United States Buyers such

whether at law or equity.
9.  Title
Subject to Section 10 (Shipping) below, title, profits and 
risks shall pass from Consignor to Buyer when the lot(s) 
are remitted to Buyer and when the full payment (includ-
ing hammer price, Buyer’s Premium and any other related 
charges) has been settled by Buyer. 
Notwithstanding the above and in the event the lots are 
shipped to Buyers, title, profits and risks pass from Con -
signor to Buyer at the destination upon tender of delivery 
provided full payment (including hammer price, Buyer’s 
Premium and any related charges) has been settled by 
Buyer and in accordance with Section 10 below. 
10.  Shipping
Lots will be temporarily admitted in Switzerland for the 
Auction Sale and shipped back at Stack’s Bowers risks to 
either Denmark or the U.S.A. immediately after the Auc-
tion Sale. Lots will then be delivered to Buyers from those 
countries. 
Relevant Shipping clauses of the Danish and U.S.A. Terms 
of Sale of Stack’s Bowers shall apply as if the Auction Sale 
had taken place respectively in Denmark or in the U.S.A. 
depending on where the lots have been shipped back by 
Stack’s Bowers after the Auction Sale. 
11. DISCLAIMER AND W ARRANTIES
NO W ARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FIT -
NESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE IS MADE OR 
IMPLIED ON ANY LOT. NO W ARRANTY , WHETHER 
EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED, IS MADE WITH RESPECT 
TO ANY LOT EXCEPT FOR W ARRANTY OF TITLE 
PROVIDED BY THE CONSIGNOR. AUCTIONEER 
IS SELLING ONLY THAT RIGHT OR TITLE TO THE 
LOT THAT THE CONSIGNOR MAY HAVE AS OF THE 
AUCTION SALE DATE. ALL LOTS ARE SOLD “ AS IS” 
AND WITH ALL FAULTS. BUYER HEREBY ASSUMES 
ALL RISKS CONCERNING AND RELATED TO THE 
GRADING, QUALITY , DESCRIPTION, CONDITION, 
AND PROVENANCE OF A LOT.
a. COINS, LISTED IN THIS CATALOG AS GRAD -
ED AND ENCAPSULATED BY PCGS, NGC, CAC 
GRADING, ANACS, ICG, PCGS CURRENCY , PMG, 
PCGS BANKNOTE GRADING, CMC OR ANY 
OTHER THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE ARE 
SOLD “ AS-IS” EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH 
HEREIN AND MAY NOT BE RETURNED FOR 
ANY REASON WHATSOEVER BY ANY BUYER. 
ALL THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE GUAR -
ANTEES, INCLUDING AUTHENTICITY , ARE THE 
SOLE RESPONSIBILITY OF THE THIRD PARTY 
GRADING SERVICE AND NOT W ARRANTIES OR 
GUARANTEES OF THE AUCTIONEER. BUYERS 
SHOULD CONTACT THESE THIRD PARTY GRAD-
ING SERVICES DIRECTLY WITH RESPECT TO 
ANY CLAIMS OR QUESTIONS THEY MAY HAVE 
CONCERNING THEIR GUARANTEES AND W AR-
RANTIES. BUYERS ACKNOWLEDGE AND AGREE 
THAT AUCTIONEER IS NOT BOUND BY OR LI -
ABLE FOR ANY OPINION OR CERTIFCATION BY 
ANY THIRD PARTY GRADING SERVICE.
b. In the case of non-certified coins, that have neither 
been examined by the Buyer prior to the Auction Sale, 
nor purchased by the Buyer or Buyer’s agent at the Auc-
tion Sale, if it is determined in a review by Stack’s Bow-
ers that there is a material error in the catalog descrip -
tion of a non-certified coin, such lot may be returned, 
provided written notice is received by Stack’s Bowers 
no later than seventy-two (72) hours of delivery of the 
lots in question, and such lots are returned and received 
by Stack’s Bowers, in their original, sealed containers, 
no later than fourteen (14) calendar days after delivery, 
in the same condition the lot(s) were delivered to the 
Buyer, time being of the essence. Non-certified coins, 
that have been either examined by the Buyer prior to 
the Auction Sale or purchased by the Buyer or Buyer’s 
agent at the Auction Sale, are not granted return privi-
leges, except for authenticity (unless described and sold 
as not authentic).
c. All non-certified coins are guaranteed to be authentic 
(unless described and sold as not authentic).
d. If an item or items are returned pursuant to the terms 
herein, they must be housed in their original, sealed 
and unopened container.
e. Late remittance or removal of any item from its origi-
nal container or third party graded holder, or altering 
a coin constitutes just cause for revocation of all return 
privileges.
f. Grading or condition of rare coins, may have a mate -
rial effect on the value of the item(s) purchased, and the 
opinion of others (including independent grading ser -
vices) may differ with the independent grading servic -
es opinion or interpretation of Stack’s Bowers. Stack’s 
Bowers shall not be bound by any prior, or subsequent 
opinion, determination or certification by any indepen-
dent grading service.
g. Questions regarding the minting of a coin as a “proof ” 
or as a “business strike” relate to the method of manu-
facture and not to authenticity.
h. All oral and written statements made by Stack’s Bow-
ers and its employees or agents (including affiliated and 
related companies) are statements of opinion only, and 
are not warranties or representations of any kind, un -
less stated otherwise stated in a specific written war -
ranty, and no employee or agent of Stack’s Bowers has 
authority to vary or alter these Terms of Sale.
i. Stack’s Bowers is acting only as Auctioneer. Title to the 
lots purchased passes directly from the Consignor to 
the Buyer. Accordingly, Stack’s Bowers does not make, 
and disclaims, any warranty of title.
j. Bidders shall have no recourse directly against the 
Consignor for any reason whatsoever, except for war -
ranties of title.

k. Bidder acknowledges that the numismatic market is

the future.

providers or consignors warrant that auctions will be 
unimpaired, uninterrupted or error free and accord -
ingly shall not be liable for any such events.
12. Waiver and Release
Bidder, for themself, their heirs, agents, successors and 
assignees, generally and specifically waives and releases, 
and forever discharges Stack’s Bowers, and its respective 
affiliates, parents, officers, directors, shareholders, agents, 
subsidiaries, employees, managers and members and 
each of them, and their respective successors and assign-
ees from any and all claims, rights, demands and causes 
of actions and suits, of whatever kind or nature, includ -
ing but not limited to claims based upon Auctioneer’s 
negligence, whether in law or equity, tort or otherwise, 
whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected 
(a “Claim”), which Bidder may assert with respect to and/
or arising out of, or in connection with any challenge to 
the authenticity of any lot purchased, the sale itself, any lot 
bid upon or consigned, and/or the auction, except where 
such Claim is otherwise expressly authorized in these 
Terms of Sale or to the extent that the application of such 
waivers and releases is in any way restricted or prohib -
ited by applicable law. It is the intention of Bidder that 
this waiver and release shall be effective as a bar to each 
and every Claim that may arise hereunder or be related to 
the Auction Sale, and Bidder hereby knowingly and vol -
untarily waives any and all rights and benefits otherwise 
conferred upon him or her by the provisions of Section 
1542 of the California Civil Code if applicable, which 
reads in full as follows:
“ A GENERAL RELEASE DOES NOT EXTEND TO 
CLAIMS WHICH THE CREDITOR OR RELEASING 
PARTY DOES NOT KNOW OR SUSPECT TO EXIST IN 
HIS OR HER FAVOR AT THE TIME OF EXECUTING 
THE RELEASE, WHICH IF KNOWN BY HIM OR HER 
MUST HAVE MATERIALLY AFFECTED HIS OR HER 
SETTLEMENT WITH THE DEBTOR OR RELEASED 
PA RT Y.”
If a Bidder or Buyer is acting as a consumer, the waiver 
and release in this section 12 shall not apply.
13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
Except as expressly provided herein, these Terms of Sale, 
the Auction Sale and any claim, dispute or controversy 
arising out of or related to these Terms of Sale, or the 
construction, interpretation, performance, enforceabil -
ity of validity of these Terms of Sale, the Auction Sale or 
any lot (a “ Dispute”) shall be determined in accordance 
with Swiss law under exclusion of possible references to 
the Swiss Private International Law Act (PILA) and under 
exclusion of the Vienna C. I S. G Convention (UN Con-
vention) and any dispute related thereto shall be settled 
under the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Can-
ton of Zürich, Switzerland, subject to appeal to the Swiss 
Federal Tribunal excluding any Dispute with a Bidder or 
Buyer residing in the United States, in which case the laws 
of the State of California shall apply, without regard to its 
principles of conflict of laws, and under the exclusive ju -
risdiction of the State and Federal courts sitting in Orange 
County, California. Notwithstanding the above, Stack’s 
Bowers reserves the right to initiate legal proceedings be-
fore any other competent court. 
14. General Terms
These Terms of Sale and the information on the Stack’s 
Bowers’ website constitute the entire agreement between 
the parties hereto on the subject matter hereof and su -
persede all other agreements, understandings, warranties 
and representations concerning the subject matter hereof. 
If any section of these Terms of Sale or any term or pro -
vision of any section is held to be invalid, void, or un -
enforceable by any court of competent jurisdiction, the 
remaining sections or terms and provisions of a section 
shall continue in full force and effect without being im -
paired or invalidated in any way.
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to vary or alter the Terms 
of Sale, either generally or with respect to specific persons 
or circumstances, in its sole discretion. Any variation or 
alteration shall be effective only if in writing and signed 
by an officer of Stack’s Bowers authorized to do so.
Stack’s Bowers reserves the right to publish photographs 
or other representations of the items sold at the Auction 
Sale on its website and in its own publications for promo-
tional purposes. 
The Auction Sale takes place under the supervision of the 
Stadtammannamt Zürich 1. Neither the Stadtammann 
nor anyone of his staff nor the City or Canton Zurich are 
in any way liable for the actions of the Auctioneer.
15. Non-English Translation
The non-English translations of these Terms of Sale (if 
any) are provided as a matter of convenience. In the event 
of a conflict, all English Terms of Sale and lot descriptions 
take precedence and are binding.
Bidding in this Auction Sale constitutes unconditional ac-
ceptance by the Bidder of the foregoing Terms of Sale.
Please note: Transparent holders in which the auction lots 
are stored are to facilitate viewing and inspection of the 
lots and ARE NOT for long-term storage.
PCGS and NGC numbers provided are for Bidder conve-
nience only, we do not guarantee their accuracy. An in -
correct PCGS or NGC number is not grounds to return 
a lot.
For PRICES REALIZED after the sale, call 1-800-458-
4646. Preliminary prices realized will also be posted on 
the Internet soon after the session closes.
